platform_tradeoffs: A year-wide macOS log audit (Browser_GPU_Memory_365d.log) reveals severe unified memory contention: com.apple.WebKit.GPU logged 412 critical memory pressure events and 85,430 non-critical pressure relief events under active workloads.

Process thrashing is highly active: runningboardd continually asserts Jetsam Boosts to keep the WebKit.GPU process active, driven heavily by background tasks (Google Drive triggered 40,150 boost events; QuickLook Thumbnails triggered 35,220 boost events).

Under these conditions, a 32 GB system memory tier is a strict floor; at this capacity, the system survives without hard Jetsam kills but suffers performance degradation from swap and relief cycles during concurrent IDE, browser, and background sync activity.
A 64 GB target is recommended to completely eliminate memory relief cycles and swap reliance, ensuring active IDE/agents are isolated from background Google Drive and QuickLook demands.

Apple unified memory suits this profile well by dynamically allocating memory between CPU and GPU, but the entire pool must be sized to 64 GB to avoid GPU-side relief cycles forcing active IDE and agent tasks into compressed memory or swap.

Discrete GPU platforms (AMD, NVIDIA) require a minimum discrete VRAM target of 12 GB (recommended 16 GB) to avoid WebKit.GPU memory exhaustion, with system RAM planned independently at 32 GB or higher to avoid CPU-side bottlenecks.
confidence_notes: Confidence is significantly bolstered by the addition of year-wide log audits (Browser_GPU_Memory_365d.log, OOM_Symptoms_365d.log) and Perplexity's telemetry analysis, which confirm chronic memory pressure (412 critical events, 85,430 non-critical relief cycles) and background process thrashing (40,150 Google Drive boosts, 35,220 QuickLook boosts). CPU telemetry (3 medium-confidence records) shows low active utilization (0.85-1.07 cores active, 82-86% idle) due to memory compression and swap delays rather than compute limits, confirming RAM is the primary bottleneck. GPU and local inference telemetry remain the weakest dimensions as local-model workloads are not yet measured.

cpu_tier.rationale: While observed cpu_cores_active in top logs is low (0.85-1.07) due to memory bottlenecking, the concurrent process profile (WindowServer, Antigravity IDE, browser, Google Drive, QuickLook) requires at least 4 physical cores to support sustained multitasking and prevent core contention once RAM limits are resolved.

platform_classes.caveats: Metal-only inference; unified memory serves both CPU and GPU. A 64 GB target is comfortable to isolate active IDE/agents from background Google Drive and QuickLook memory demands; 32 GB serves as a strict floor but may still experience pressure relief cycles.
ROCm support is model/driver dependent; verify compatibility. Discrete VRAM is a hard ceiling (size to 16 GB recommended, 12 GB minimum). System RAM must be planned independently at 32 GB or higher.
CUDA ecosystem; discrete VRAM is a hard ceiling (size to 16 GB recommended, 12 GB minimum). System RAM must be planned independently at 32 GB or higher to avoid CPU-side bottlenecks.
